subject: Re: Using links
body:
Hi Both,

We are also using links to WARP and it is working fine, however, warp does 
not seem to recognise new links added - has there been a solution for this?
Best wishes,
Daniel

On Monday, 22 October 2018 at 09:28:29 UTC+1 teg...@gmail.com wrote:

> Dear Arne,
>
> file links working is great news! I was just thinking of them as a 
> solution to this Twitter thread 
> <https://twitter.com/WimJHH/status/1053280921057718272>, but you're miles 
> ahead! Let me check why file system events work differently for links 
> compared to normal files. Meanwhile, changing the input file extension to a 
> different format and then back might be less annoying than restarting the 
> whole program.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
>
> On Monday, October 22, 2018 at 9:57:05 AM UTC+2, Arne Moeller wrote:
>>
>> Dear Dimitry,
>>
>> many thanks for Warp - its fantastic and we use it regularly. 
>>
>> For data storage we like to confine folders to either large or small 
>> files - which works quite well if we generate links in our processing 
>> directory pointing at an isolated Raw-Data folder. 
>> Warp has no problem in seeing the links and also processing the files 
>> correctly - however, it does not recognize if new links are added. 
>> Therefore, we cannot before on-the-fly-processing anymore. 
>>
>> The way we are using it is that we have a script moving the raw-mics from 
>> the scope to the main storage and automatically generating a link for every 
>> new file once it has fully appeared on the storage. 
>>
>> Right now we have to regularly restart Warp - actually we to 
>> "fake-change" a parameter in addition - in order for Warp to see the new 
>> images appearing. 
>>
>> I am happy to provide more information if necessary.
>>
>>
>> Your help would be greatly appreciated,
>>
>> many thanks
>> cheers
>> Arne
>>
>>
>>
>> MPI-Biophysics Frankfurt
>>
>
